input.prompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

data.content:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

arguments.command: mkdir -p /tmp/sv-e503082b682345cf901f0a60d83b64c6/McpServer && cd /tmp/sv-e503082b682345cf901f0a60d83b64c6/McpServer && dotnet new console -n McpServer --force -o . 2>&1 | tail -3

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server project with:
1. A tool class wrapping a REST API using HttpClient
2. Program.cs with stdio transport
3. Explanation of how DI works for MCP tools

Let me create a practical example. I'll set up a working project structure.

toolArgs.old_str:

Console.WriteLine("Hello, World!");

toolCalls.name: edit

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-e503082b682345cf901f0a60d83b64c6/McpServer/Program.cs b/tmp/sv-e503082b682345cf901f0a60d83b64c6/McpServer/Program.cs
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-e503082b682345cf901f0a60d83b64c6/McpServer/Program.cs
+++ b/tmp/sv-e503082b682345cf901f0a60d83b64c6/McpServer/Program.cs
@@ -1,2 +1,20 @@
-﻿Console.WriteLine("Hello, World!");
+using McpServer;
+using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
+using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
+
+var builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder(args);
+
+builder.Services
+    // Register a named HttpClient for WeatherTools — IHttpClientFactory creates
+    // and injects an HttpClient instance each time the tool class is constructed.
+    .AddHttpClient<WeatherTools>()
+
+    // Register the MCP server with stdio transport and discover all [McpServerTool]
+    // methods in the current assembly.
+    .Services
+    .AddMcpServer()
+    .WithStdioServerTransport()
+    .WithToolsFromAssembly();
+
+await builder.Build().RunAsync();

+
+    // Register the MCP server with stdio transport and discover all [McpServerTool]

+    .WithStdioServerTransport()
+    .WithToolsFromAssembly();
+
+await builder.Build().RunAsync();
